column58: Customer asked if he was using 4g sim or 5g and here RM said that customer's sim was 4g. Then customer asked what can be done to use the sim in 5g where RM said that customer will be notified when the 5g will be launched which was wrong information. Here RM should have clarified if customer wanted to use the 5g service or convert the sim to 4g to 5g which was missing. Moreover as per our process, customer can use the 5g service in some selected 5G location using the selected device which wasn't informed by RM. So overall we considering this transaction as CE.

column70: During the onboarding call, the RM committed to sending his official RM confirmation sms through "GP Info" masking, and the customer agreed to receive the information. However, upon verification, it was found that the RM did not send the required RM confirmation Push SMS which was necessary to deliver as promised. As a result, the commitment made to the customer was not fulfilled, making this transaction a Critical Error (CE) due to False Commitment.
